message:
test(fcp-core): pin SecretAccessToken Debug-redaction + predicate truth tables + serde shape (flywheel_connectors-5bqje)

Bead asks for RegistryToken Display + serde pinning. No type literally
named RegistryToken exists in fcp-core. Other tokens already pinned:
LeaseToken (lease_token_display.rs + lease_token_format_invariants.rs),
CapabilityToken (capability_token_display_roundtrip.rs via CapabilityId),
ConsentToken (consent_token_display_roundtrip.rs).

Residual unpinned token: SecretAccessToken at secret.rs:413 — the
security-critical short-lived single-use token granting access to a
secret. NO Display impl (intentionally — never log a token); the
"Display" surface is the redacted Debug impl.

Coverage: Debug redacts authorization bytes (loud security sentinel:
authorization MUST NOT leak via {:?} log scrapes — verified with
diverse byte payloads including CANARY-MAGIC). is_expired boundary
(now < / == / > expires_at; equality IS expired per >= rule),
is_exhausted truth table, is_valid requires BOTH not-expired AND
not-exhausted. record_use returns false when exhausted, increments
use_count from 0 on first call, refuses to overshoot max_uses.
remaining_uses uses saturating_sub (does NOT underflow). authorization()
helper returns inner bytes. JSON+CBOR full round-trip preserves all
10 fields including the (currently-serialized) authorization (pin
loudly so future skip_serializing is caught — could be intentional
for security or accidental data loss). Each new() generates distinct
token_id (audit-correlation independence). max_uses=0 immediately-
exhausted edge sentinel.

15 tests green via CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p1-5bqje cargo test -p
fcp-core --test registry_token_display.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
